title: Nephrodium dissidens
taxonomy: Nephrodium dissidens is classified within the fern family, specifically within the genus Nephrolepis. However, some sources may refer to it as Nephrolepis dissidens. It belongs to the family Nephrolepidaceae. Furthermore, it is also known to be part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies. The classification of Nephrodium dissidens is subject to some variation in different taxonomic systems.

intro: Nephrodium dissidens, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
